summary: Moved several fighter HSD_ObjAllocData .bss objects out of asm/melee/ft/ftparts.s and into src/melee/ft/fighter.c, replacing fake pointer-arithmetic indexing from lbl_80458FD0 with explicit object allocator globals. This cleaned up Fighter_800679B0 and Fighter_Unload_8006DABC by removing a weird uninitialized-pointer fakematch helper and using the correct allocator symbols directly. The Fighter struct was padded so sizeof(Fighter) could replace a hardcoded 0x23ec allocation size, and the unload callback prototype was corrected to take void* user_data.
what_changed: Removed .bss definitions lbl_80459028, lbl_80459054, lbl_80459080, and lbl_804590AC from asm/melee/ft/ftparts.s.
Declared the same HSD_ObjAllocData globals directly in fighter.c alongside existing lbl_80458FD0 and lbl_80458FFC.
Changed Fighter_800679B0 allocator initialization from pointer arithmetic on &lbl_80458FD0, such as &lbl_80458FD0 + 2, to explicit symbols lbl_80459028, lbl_80459054, and lbl_80459080.
Changed the main fighter allocation initialization from hardcoded size 0x23ec to sizeof(Fighter).
Removed an inline sub_func fakematch that used an uninitialized Fighter* to obtain matching codegen for Fighter_Unload_8006DABC.
Rewrote Fighter_Unload_8006DABC to accept void* user_data, cast it to Fighter*, read x4_fighterKind normally, and call ft_OnUserDataRemove[kind] if present.

Changed HSD_ObjFree calls in Fighter_Unload_8006DABC from computed objAllocData indices to direct allocator globals: lbl_804590AC, lbl_80459028, lbl_80459054, lbl_80459080, lbl_80458FFC, and lbl_80458FD0.
Added filler_x23B8 padding to Fighter so the struct size reaches the allocation size boundary used by sizeof(Fighter).
Updated the fighter.h declaration of Fighter_Unload_8006DABC from Fighter* to void* user_data after review feedback.
smart_moves: Fixed a .bss ownership problem by moving HSD_ObjAllocData objects into the C translation unit that uses them, instead of pretending they are contiguous via pointer arithmetic from lbl_80458FD0.
Replaced magic allocation size 0x23ec with sizeof(Fighter), but only after padding the Fighter struct to make that expression match the expected size.
Removed a fragile fakematch based on an uninitialized pointer and compiler warning, making Fighter_Unload_8006DABC readable while preserving allocator behavior through explicit symbols.
Used direct HSD_ObjFree allocator addresses, which documents which pool each fighter subobject belongs to better than computed offsets like objAllocData[(0xDC / 40)].
Aligned the unload callback API with a generic user_data callback shape by taking void* and casting internally.
decomp_lessons: If a sequence of .bss objects is being accessed through pointer arithmetic only because some labels still live in asm, consider moving all related objects into C so the compiler emits the same layout and the code can name each object directly.
A matching workaround that depends on an uninitialized local is a strong signal that data layout or symbol ownership is wrong, not that the function logic needs to remain strange.
When replacing hardcoded object sizes with sizeof, first make the struct definition represent the allocated storage, including known tail padding or filler fields.
Explicit allocator globals can be preferable to array-index fakematches when the original data is a set of independent BSS symbols with 0x2C-sized HSD_ObjAllocData entries.
Callback signatures may need to use void* user_data even when the implementation immediately casts to a specific struct type; match the caller/framework contract in headers.
naming_conventions: Kept existing lbl_80458FD0, lbl_80458FFC, lbl_80459028, lbl_80459054, lbl_80459080, and lbl_804590AC placeholder names because no semantic names were evidenced.
Used filler_x23B8 for explicit Fighter tail padding, following the offset-based filler naming style present in decomp structs.
Reviewer suggested naming the void* parameter user_data in Fighter_Unload_8006DABC; the final header uses void* user_data.
Local variable kind was introduced for fp->x4_fighterKind before indexing ft_OnUserDataRemove, improving readability without introducing an unsupported semantic rename.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted .bss labels from ftparts.s once they were represented as C globals in fighter.c.
Preserved BSS ordering by declaring HSD_ObjAllocData globals in fighter.c after lbl_80458FD0 and lbl_80458FFC.
Converted allocator initialization from &lbl_80458FD0 + N to named globals to avoid depending on implicit contiguity.
Converted HSD_ObjFree calls from objAllocData index expressions based on byte offsets divided by 40 to direct labels matching the relevant allocator pools.
Eliminated a decomp.me-style weird match involving an inline helper, an uninitialized pointer, and a compiler warning.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested the prototype use void Fighter_Unload_8006DABC(void* user_data); rather than void* fp; comment included 'but whatever'.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Responded 'Fixed', and the final diff shows the parameter name as user_data.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbl_80458FD0 or the other fighter HSD_ObjAllocData globals to semantic allocator names.
Check whether Fighter size and tail padding around x23B8 were later refined beyond filler_x23B8.
Search for remaining HSD_ObjAllocData pointer-arithmetic fakematches in other systems that could be cleaned up by moving BSS symbols into C.
Look for callback prototypes taking Fighter* where the engine actually passes void* user_data.
